Overall Score

              
Combined Research Fit

              
6 policy comparison reports contributed to this score.

            

            

              
        

          
          
        

        
            

          

          

            

              
Strongest Areas

              
  • Risk Alignment (50)
  • Method Alignment (47)
  • Deployment Alignment (46)

            

            

              
Watch Areas

              
  • Governance Alignment (22)
  • Problem Alignment (30)
  • Stakeholder Alignment (38)

            

          

        

        

          
Best Next Move

          
  • Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
  • Establish networks and organize information infrastructure among various interested and responsible parties domestically and internationally, in order to promote conservation and sustainable resource use of the coral reef ecosystem. MOE will collect and organize information on coral reef researches and conservation efforts and strengthen organizational and systematic structures.
  • Future work could involve integrating continual learning techniques and using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required, to establish networks and organize information infrastructure among various interested and responsible parties domestically and internationally.
  • Automated Detection of Dolphin Whistles With Convolutional Networks and Transfer Learning, Summary for Policymakers of the Thematic Assessment Report on the Underlying Causes of Biodiversity Loss and the Determinants of Transformative Change and Options for Achieving the 2050 Vision for Biodiversity
  • All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset. Binary cross-entropy loss function was applied to each unit in the network output as a loss function.
  • Developing a user-friendly GUI to package the model, making it accessible to non-professional stakeholders and conservation volunteers. MOE will plan exhibitions that highlight the value of coral reefs and call action for its conservation.
  • To better connect the research capability to policy use, the researchers could emphasize how their work on improving deep models for underwater ecological images can contribute to advanced AI capabilities in the Marine Corps.

                   · data · data_quality · c021 · p.0
                  
    Images captured with different cameras and varying distances from the object, resulting in inconsistencies in image quality, color, and resolution.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · data · data_quality · c002 · p.1
                  
    However, this approach has significant limitations, including high labor demands, low classification efficiency, and inconsistencies in data quality. These challenges arise because the entire process is highly dependent on the expertise and subjective judgment of human experts (Done et al., 2017; Figuerola-Ferrando et al., 2024)

    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c017 · p.119
                  
    Conflict Analysis and Reallocation Opportunities in the Framework of Marine Spatial Planning: A Novel, Spatially Explicit Bayesian Belief Network Approach for Artisanal Fishing and Aquaculture.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c018 · p.338
                  
    Machine Learning for Benthic Sand and Maerl Classification and Coverage Estimation in Coastal Areas Around the Maltese Islands.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c003 · p.2
                  
    The design and reporting of this study followed the guidelines established by the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) framework [105].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c014 · p.1
                  
    The biomagnification parameters for greater trophic species and their effects on larger marine food webs are unexplored [25]. Concurrent consumption of micro-particles can change chromo­ somal, resulting in sterility, overweight, and cancers [130,151]. The presence of substances that mimic estrogen in women’s bodies has been linked to an increased risk of prostate cancer. Because humans are exposed to MPs through their meals, with a larger concentration seen in marine food items, this constitutes a substantial concern for consumer protection [160].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.1
                  
    Research revealed that PVC, PE, PET, and PA66 contact and consumption affected the immu­ nological function, antioxidant properties, mineralization, and energy consumption in Tubastrea aurea.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.2
                  
    Syakti et al. [150] assessed the consequences of three intentness (0.05, 0.1, and 0.15 g/L) and dimensions (100, 100–200, and 200–500 m) upon Acropora formosa’s viability.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.2
                  
    Whenever the future effects of two kinds of MP (63–125 m, 1 and 10 mg/L) above a 96-h exposition were evaluated, the hexacorallia reef Zoanthus sociatus was much more sensitive to PVC MP than LDPE,
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.2
                  
    Lanctˆot et al. [86] studied the effects of four weeks of exposure to 5 ± 1 particles/mL (mean ± SD; median = 4.67) and 70 ± 33 parti­ cles/mL PE MP on the stony coral Stylophora pistillata.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.3
                  
    Recent research on MP and its effects on coral organisms have been concentrated on scientific tests at concentrations greater than those observed in actual reef settings.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c021 · p.45
                  
    used nine distinct kinds of 3–5-millimeter MP to investigate the composition and features of MP surface pathogens in 4 coral reef locations.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c022 · p.2
                  
    Therefore different studies acknowledged several gaps, including MPs presence in wild corals [126], active or passive mechanisms of MP on coral tissue [143], stony corals’ in-situ reactions to MP [171], MPs psychological effect on corals [124] where other studies focused on prevalence, distribution, concentration, pollution of MPs on corals (Table 4).
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c022 · p.3
                  
    In this section, we aim to fill this gap by examining the policies and strategies adopted by different agencies and the existing recommenda­ tions for reducing plastic pollution.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c028 · p.0
                  
    Several organizations and governments have taken different initia­ tives to ban or reuse or recycle plastics to mitigate adverse impacts [34].

Bridge Actions

    Establish networks and organize information infrastructure among various interested and responsible parties domestically and internationally, in order to promote conservation and sustainable resource use of the coral reef ecosystem.           Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · recommendations · implementation_guidance · c013 · p.2
                  
    MOE will collect and organize information on coral reef researches and conservation efforts and strengthen organizational and systematic structures. MOE will fully utilize data from their programs such as National Survey on the Natural Environment and Monitoring Sites 1000. MAFF will collect and organize scientific information on rare species, and develop conservation and management plans.

Policy Drill-Down

      
The dashboard above is combined per research paper. The panels below keep the policy-by-policy field results, rationales, and chunk-level evidence for anyone who wants to trace an insight back to the source text.

      

        
        

          
            

              
Policy Comparison

              

              
The research field focuses on advanced AI-driven image recognition algorithms but lacks explicit consideration of data collection policies and legitimizing measures required by the policy stance. Additionally, detailed habitat variation data collected in the research could be better leveraged for targeted policy analysis. The research field provides a clear statement about open-source licensing, but this is not explicitly required or utilized in the combined policy stance. The research field highlights accessibility through open-sourcing data and tools, which aligns with the policy's aim to make AI technology directly usable by public users.

            

            

              
Pair-Level Fit

              
        

          
          
        

        
            

          
          

            

              
Field Calls10

              
Shared Features12

              
Policy Gaps9

              
Bridge Actions14

            

            

              
        

          
Shared Features

          
Shared Features

          
Highlights that matched this one policy.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    Use of advanced AI-driven image recognition algorithms for marine biodiversity analysis.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c009 · p.3
                  
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio. For experiments testing transfer learning capability, the dataset was split differently... All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset (Oquab et al., 2024). Subsequently, all models were fine-tuned using PyTorch.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c002 · p.1
                  
    The project utilizes advanced AI-driven image recognition algorithms to analyze marine biodiversity, and employs big data analytics to process vast oceanographic datasets.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Both the research field and the combined policy stance mention open-source aspects.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · licensing · c002 · p.2
                  
    This paper will focus on the model established and the legality of AI technology for other research teams’ reference when using AI to conduct scientific research.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Open access to data and tools

Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Requirements this policy asked for that the research still misses.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    Data collection policies and legitimizing measures not explicitly addressed in the research field.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c008 · p.1
                  
    The sub-project teams should follow the Working Principles of Data and Model Governance established by sub-project team (D). Specifically, when sub-project teams understand the content of the Data Collection Policy, aside from executing the legitimizing measures, they should confirm whether intellectual property rights and classified and sensitive information are involved in data sources and fill out the material inspection form.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The policies do not explicitly mention or require open-source licensing for data collection.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    'Survey' method mentioned in policy evidence is not present in the research field.

                   · methods · method_type · c016 · p.1

                   · data · collection_protocols · c008 · p.1

Specifically, when sub-project teams understand the content of the Data Collection Policy, aside from executing the legitimizing measures, they should confirm whether intellectual property rights and classified and sensitive information are involved in data sources and fill out the material inspection form.

                  Data · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Accessibility

            Data
          

          
The research field highlights accessibility through open-sourcing data and tools, which aligns with the policy's aim to make AI technology directly usable by public users.

          

            Transparency · 1Auditability · 0Accessibility · 1Reproducibility · 1Compliance · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Open access to data and tools

Collection Protocols

            Data
          

          
The research field focuses on advanced AI-driven image recognition algorithms but lacks explicit consideration of data collection policies and legitimizing measures required by the policy stance. Additionally, detailed habitat variation data collected in the research could be better leveraged for targeted policy analysis.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Accessibility · 0Reproducibility · 0Compliance · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Use of advanced AI-driven image recognition algorithms for marine biodiversity analysis.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c009 · p.3
                  
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio. For experiments testing transfer learning capability, the dataset was split differently... All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset (Oquab et al., 2024). Subsequently, all models were fine-tuned using PyTorch.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c002 · p.1
                  
    The project utilizes advanced AI-driven image recognition algorithms to analyze marine biodiversity, and employs big data analytics to process vast oceanographic datasets.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1

    Data collection policies and legitimizing measures not explicitly addressed in the research field.

                   · data · collection_protocols · c008 · p.1

Specifically, when sub-project teams understand the content of the Data Collection Policy, aside from executing the legitimizing measures, they should confirm whether intellectual property rights and classified and sensitive information are involved in data sources and fill out the material inspection form.

Techniques

            Methods
          

          
The research field focuses on advanced image recognition techniques, which aligns with the policy's emphasis on AI-driven algorithms. However, there are gaps in integrating IoT technologies and engaging stakeholders, which could be addressed through targeted actions.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 0Integration Feasibility · 0

                   · methods · techniques · c002 · p.1
                  
    Underwater transect survey method, automated image classification based on universal assessment standards
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c005 · p.1
                  
    Shao et al. (2024) evaluated the performance of several SOTA deep learning architectures for multi-label classification...resulting in a match ratio of 63.33% and a macro F1 score of 81.74%
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c002 · p.1

Workflows

            Methods
          

          
The research field shares some common ground with the policy stance in using AI-driven image recognition algorithms and processing large datasets. However, there are gaps in meeting policy requirements for cross-country comparisons and utilizing all research capabilities.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 0Integration Feasibility · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Use of advanced AI-driven image recognition algorithms
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c002 · p.1
                  
    Volunteer divers document coral reef conditions through text, photos, or videos; Automated image classification using deep learning models
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c008 · p.1
                  
    Divide input images into patches of size 512 × 512 pixels, flatten and pass through linear projection with positional embeddings. Process tokenized representations through Transformer blocks with learnable class token. Append classification decoder for multi-label prediction.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Processing large datasets
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c003 · p.1
                  
    Establish an open-source dataset based on underwater photo transect surveys, annotate images by domain experts, apply LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Cross-country comparisons and empirical surveys
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Transfer learning capabilities across seasons
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Evaluating computational efficiency of different low-rank numbers (r)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Explicitly mention cross-country comparisons and empirical surveys in research methodology to align with policy expectations
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Explore how transfer learning capabilities can be applied across different marine environments or seasons for better policy integration
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Problem · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Primary Goal

            Problem
          

          
The research field focuses on a specific AI application for coral reef condition analysis, while the policies have broader objectives including sustainable practices and intellectual property considerations. The research capability could be better connected to policy use by explicitly addressing these aspects.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Use of AI for marine research
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · primary_goal · c002 · p.1
                  
    The team employs AI to assist their research work, aiming to develop and integrate various Taiwanese technologies to create a ver­ satile underwater exploration system for marine exploration and envi­ ronmental analysis.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Embedding sustainable practices and responsible data use into marine research protocols.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · primary_goal · c001 · p.1
                  
    The proposed model explicitly supports the long-term objectives of the UN Ocean Decade by embedding sustainable practices and responsible data use into marine research protocols.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Addressing the challenges of AI in intellectual property and ensuring fairness
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Multi-label classification of coral reef conditions under multi-temporal and multi-spatial settings.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Explicitly state how the proposed model embeds sustainable practices and responsible data use.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · primary_goal · c001 · p.1
                  
    The proposed model explicitly supports the long-term objectives of the UN Ocean Decade by embedding sustainable practices and responsible data use into marine research protocols.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Explore how the multi-label classification capability can address intellectual property challenges in AI.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Problem Statement

            Problem
          

          
The research field and combined policy stance share a common focus on marine environment issues but lack direct alignment on specific methods or capabilities.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Both the research and policy documents address marine environment issues.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c002 · p.1
                  
    resources, recent research has shown that rapid changes in the marine environment have caused a decline in marine biodiversity. The alteration of ocean temperatures leads to behavioral changes in marine creatures.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The policy document mentions AI infringement on intellectual property rights, which is not addressed in the research field.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c003 · p.1
                  
    AI infringement on intellectual property rights, whether AI can count as an inventor or whether AI-generated works are protected by copyright
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research field discusses the limitation of conventional deep models in processing underwater ecological images, but this capability is not used or mentioned in the policy evidence.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    To better connect the research capability to policy use, researchers could explicitly discuss how improved deep learning models for underwater images could help monitor marine biodiversity changes mentioned in the policy.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c002 · p.1
                  
    resources, recent research has shown that rapid changes in the marine environment have caused a decline in marine biodiversity. The alteration of ocean temperatures leads to behavioral changes in marine creatures.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Problem Types

            Problem
          

          
The research field 'problem_types' aligns with the combined policy stance on topical overlap but not on specific governance aspects like legal compliance.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0

    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.0

                   · stakeholders · actors · c014 · p.1

    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
            

          

        

        
        

          
            

              
Policy Comparison

              

              
The research field focuses on developing AI models for coral reef image classification, while the policy evidence discusses establishing task groups for AI implementation. While there's shared interest in AI use, the specific methods and implementations differ. The research field and policy evidence both touch upon challenges related to AI, but they focus on different aspects (processing complex images vs. lacking advanced capabilities). The research field and policy documents share a common focus on monitoring, but this capability is not yet connected to the decision support needs highlighted in policies.

            

            

              
Pair-Level Fit

              
        

          
          
        

        
            

          
          

            

              
Field Calls5

              
Shared Features5

              
Policy Gaps3

              
Bridge Actions5

            

            

              
        

          
Shared Features

          
Shared Features

          
Highlights that matched this one policy.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    Use of AI/machine learning for coral reef conservation
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c002 · p.1
                  
    Volunteer divers document coral reef conditions through text, photos, or videos; Automated image classification using deep learning models
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c004 · p.13
                  
    Establish AI task groups (AITG) across the Service to support commanders in identifying their use cases, acting as the AI advisor, and serving as the key link between Headquarters Marine Corps, Fleet Marine Force, and supporting establishments. The AITGs will be responsible for execution of the IPLAN to this strategy.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Both the research field and policy evidence highlight challenges related to AI processing and capabilities.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.4
                  
    AI continues to evolve rapidly, creating challenges for the Service in areas such as doctrine, organization, leadership, materiel, training and education, personnel, facilities, and policy development. Data management is the largest and most impactful set of challenges facing AI employment today.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c002 · p.1
                  
    The Marine Corps lacks advanced AI capabilities and a skilled workforce proficient in AI, hindering their ability to gain a decisive information advantage during competition in modern warfare.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Both the research field and policy documents mention 'monitoring' as a key aspect.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Both the research field and policy evidence emphasize advanced AI capabilities.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · primary_goal · c001 · p.5
                  
    Empower Marines with advanced AI capabilities -- supported by informed leadership, strategic partnerships, a skilled workforce, and lean governance -- to enhance lethality and efficacy to ensure a decisive information advantage during competition in modern warfare.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · problem · primary_goal · c002 · p.1
                  
    Empower Marines with advanced AI capabilities to enhance lethality and efficacy for a decisive information advantage during competition in modern warfare.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Involvement of multiple actors

Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Policy Requirements Not Covered

          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c004 · p.13
                  
    Establish AI task groups (AITG) across the Service to support commanders in identifying their use cases, acting as the AI advisor, and serving as the key link between Headquarters Marine Corps, Fleet Marine Force, and supporting establishments. The AITGs will be responsible for execution of the IPLAN to this strategy.

                  Problem · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Primary Goal

            Problem
          

          
The research field's primary goal does not directly align with the objectives outlined in the combined policy stance, as it focuses on coral reef classification rather than enhancing lethality and efficacy in warfare.

                  Stakeholders · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Actors

            Stakeholders
          

          
The research field 'actors' shares a common feature with the policies, namely the involvement of multiple actors. However, the specific names of researchers in the research field are not relevant to the policy's requirements.

    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c002 · p.1
                  
    Vision Empower Marines with advanced AI capabilities -- supported by informed leadership, strategic partnerships, a skilled workforce, and lean governance -- to enhance lethality and efficacy to ensure a decisive information advantage during competition in modern warfare.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
            

          

        

        
        

          
            

              
Policy Comparison

              

              
The research field shares the goal of improving efficiency but lacks explicit connection to the policy's focus on area-based management tools. The research field directly addresses a challenge highlighted in the policy evidence but is not explicitly leveraged to meet the policy's stated need for comprehensive and actionable data. The research field and policy documents share a common focus on 'monitoring', but the research could better align with policy by explicitly connecting this capability to broader conservation and governance objectives.

            

            

              
Pair-Level Fit

              
        

          
          
        

        
            

          
          

            

              
Field Calls5

              
Shared Features5

              
Policy Gaps1

              
Bridge Actions4

            

            

              
        

          
Shared Features

          
Shared Features

          
Highlights that matched this one policy.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    Improved efficiency in image labeling
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c001 · p.1
                  
    Increase the area of coral reefs under effective management, protection, and conservation through Area-based Management Tools (ABMT), highly and fully protected areas, Marine Protected Areas (MPA), Other Effective Conservation Measures (OECM), and Indigenous and traditional territories and local community stewardship.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Addressing challenges in processing underwater ecological images
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c003 · p.1
                  
    The lack of comprehensive and actionable data on coral reef health hinders effective management and decision-making.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Both the research field and policy documents mention 'monitoring' as a key aspect.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Both the research field and combined policy stance aim to improve understanding and management of coral reef conditions.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · primary_goal · c003 · p.1
                  
    Strengthen coral reef monitoring to provide actionable data for informed decision-making and policy support.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Both the research and policy documents mention international collaboration in coral reef conservation.

Research Capabilities Not Used

          
Research Capabilities Not Used

          
Research strengths this policy does not yet capitalize on.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    High F1 scores not mentioned in policy
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Potential of deep models to improve coral reef health data processing
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    'Monitoring' is mentioned in the research field but not explicitly linked to any governance or conservation objectives as seen in policy documents.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The research's multi-label classification capability could help identify specific reef conditions, but this is not explicitly linked to policy needs in the provided evidence.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The research field lists specific individuals involved in the study, but this information is not utilized or required by the policy evidence.

Bridge Actions

          
Bridge Actions

          
Concrete moves that would make this policy fit stronger.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    Explicitly mention high F1 scores in policy to emphasize model's accuracy
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c001 · p.1
                  
    Increase the area of coral reefs under effective management, protection, and conservation through Area-based Management Tools (ABMT), highly and fully protected areas, Marine Protected Areas (MPA), Other Effective Conservation Measures (OECM), and Indigenous and traditional territories and local community stewardship.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Explore how advanced deep learning techniques can enhance the quality and quantity of underwater ecological image processing, contributing to better coral reef health data.

    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1

                   · problem · problem_statement · c003 · p.1

                  Problem · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Primary Goal

            Problem
          

          
The research field focuses on improving reef condition assessment, while the policies emphasize broader conservation goals and data-driven decision-making. Better connecting these two would enhance policy relevance.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0

    Both the research field and combined policy stance aim to improve understanding and management of coral reef conditions.

            Stakeholders

Policy Comparison

              

              
This section highlights the shared features between the research field and policy documents, as well as the gaps in coverage. The research capabilities not used by the policies are also identified, along with potential bridge actions to connect the two. The research field focuses on underwater imaging and data collection methods, while the policy documents emphasize the importance of validation, transparency, and equitable access. The two fields have some shared features, but there are gaps in coverage and alignment. The research field highlights inconsistencies in data quality and imbalanced distribution of the dataset, which are not explicitly addressed in the policy documents. The policy documents focus on the need for efficient systems and reducing costs, but do not provide specific requirements or expectations for addressing these issues.

            

            

              
Pair-Level Fit

              
        

          
          
        

        
            

          
          

            

              
Field Calls20

              
Shared Features32

              
Policy Gaps30

              
Bridge Actions35

            

            

              
        

          
Shared Features

          
Shared Features

          
Highlights that matched this one policy.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    Underwater surveys at 15 dive sites at Koh Tao, Thailand

    Fieldwork conducted in Koh Tao
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Images labeled according to universal standards used in citizen science-based conservation programs
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Underwater photo-transect survey method, Olympus Tough TG-6 underwater camera with manual white balance

                   · data · data_quality · c021 · p.0

  • 
          
    Imbalanced distribution of the dataset

Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Policy Requirements Not Covered

Research strengths this policy does not yet capitalize on.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    255 Fish and Fisheries, 2026 validation, (2) robust train-test data splitting, (3) statistical test for comparisons and (4) ground truth validation.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    257 Fish and Fisheries, 2026 et al. 2024; Rasal et al. 2024), building on earlier examples from conservation research (Garnett et al. 2007; Waylen et al. 2013; Biggs et al. 2017; Balfour et al. 2019).
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio. For experiments testing transfer learning capability, the dataset was split differently...
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c009 · p.3
                  
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio. For experiments testing transfer learning capability, the dataset was split differently... All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset (Oquab et al., 2024). Subsequently, all models were fine-tuned using PyTorch.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Field images collected during dry and wet seasons at 15 diving sites in Koh Tao, Thailand
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    High labor demands, low classification efficiency
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · data_quality · c002 · p.1
                  
    However, this approach has significant limitations, including high labor demands, low classification efficiency, and inconsistencies in data quality. These challenges arise because the entire process is highly dependent on the expertise and subjective judgment of human experts (Done et al., 2017; Figuerola-Ferrando et al., 2024)
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Imbalanced distribution of the dataset
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    •

                  Data · 5 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Accessibility

            Data
          

          
The research field's focus on accessibility aligns with the policy stance, but there is a gap in explicitly mentioning protocols for authorized access.

          

            Transparency · 1Auditability · 0Accessibility · 1Reproducibility · 0Compliance · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The dataset and code are open-sourced for accessibility.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Use interoperable data platforms and repositories to ensure transparency and accessibility in marine AI research.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Explicit protocols with mechanism for authorized access
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    •

    Direct collaborations with local conservation communities or research institutes could provide higher-quality images for further transferability testing.

Collection Protocols

            Data
          

          
The research field focuses on underwater imaging and data collection methods, while the policy documents emphasize the importance of validation, transparency, and equitable access. The two fields have some shared features, but there are gaps in coverage and alignment.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Accessibility · 0Reproducibility · 1Compliance · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Images labeled according to universal standards used in citizen science-based conservation programs
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Underwater photo-transect survey method, Olympus Tough TG-6 underwater camera with manual white balance
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c006 · p.1
                  
    Underwater photo-transect survey method, Olympus Tough TG-6 underwater camera with manual white balance
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Planning stages of a research project, citizen science contributions
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Validation emerged as another critical area, emphasising the importance of reliable and interpretable AI models that stakeholders can trust.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2

Subsequently, all models were fine-tuned using PyTorch.

Data Quality

            Data
          

          
The research field highlights inconsistencies in data quality and imbalanced distribution of the dataset, which are not explicitly addressed in the policy documents. The policy documents focus on the need for efficient systems and reducing costs, but do not provide specific requirements or expectations for addressing these issues.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Accessibility · 0Reproducibility · 0Compliance · 0

Licensing

            Data
          

          
The research field does not explicitly address the licensing requirements mentioned in the policy documents, but it can be seen as a step towards providing open-source alternatives to proprietary systems.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Accessibility · 0Reproducibility · 0Compliance · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Open-source
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Proprietary systems
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Open-source AI models, cloud-based solutions and cost-effective sensor technologies can provide alternatives to expensive proprietary systems
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Develop open-source AI models and cloud-based solutions to provide alternatives to expensive proprietary systems
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Impact · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Ecological Impact

            Impact
          

          
The proposed DINOv2-LoRA model improves efficiency and reduces errors, which could support the adoption of trustworthy AI systems in marine research and fisheries.

          

            Sustainability · 0Economic Impact · 0Ecological Impact · 1Social Impact · 0Institutions · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The proposed DINOv2-LoRA model improves efficiency by reducing labeling time from five seconds to 0.02 seconds.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The model reduces errors compared to inexperienced volunteers, with high F1 scores.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The environmental impact of some AI models depends on the amount of training required and the availability of renewable energy.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The broader effects or expected outcomes of the work on society, environment, policy, or economy.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The model's high F1 scores and efficiency improvements could be used to support marine research and fisheries.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Developing trustworthy AI systems for marine research and fisheries that align with international goals.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Limitations · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Known Gaps

            Limitations
          

          
The research field highlights the need for future work to address known gaps in AI application, which aligns with policy requirements. However, the research capabilities not used by the policy stance are those that focus on broader regions outside the Indo-Pacific.

          

            Transparency · 1Auditability · 0Safety · 0Technical Maturity · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Persistent challenges include the lack of robust validation frameworks and the difficulty of applying AI effectively in data-limited settings.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The adaptability of the model to broader regions outside the Indo-Pacific remains unexplored. Future research that uses coral reef images collected from other regions is essential to further evaluate the transferability of the proposed DINOv2-LoRA.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · known_gaps · c021 · p.1
                  
    The adaptability of the model to broader regions outside the Indo-Pacific remains unexplored. Future research that uses coral reef images collected from other regions is essential to further evaluate the transferability of the proposed DINOv2-LoRA.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Limitations Summary

            Limitations
          

          
The research field's limitations in addressing imbalanced datasets are not explicitly addressed in the policy documents, which highlights the need to bridge this gap by providing training and infrastructure for workers.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Safety · 1Technical Maturity · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0
                  
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, as shown in Figure 11, where the labels are distributed non-uniformly across classes, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Concerns that automation might displace jobs, particularly where access to technical training and digital infrastructure is limited.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c005 · p.251
                  
    The uptake of AI technologies is often constrained by high initial investment costs, limited access to appropriate infrastructure, multidisciplinary experts and a lack of digital literacy within the sector. Many operators perceive such systems as opaque and detached from local ecological knowledge and socio-­economic realities (Wing and Woodward 2024).
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The limitations of the dataset used in this study.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0
                  
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, as shown in Figure 11, where the labels are distributed non-uniformly across classes, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Addressing concerns about job displacement by providing training and infrastructure for workers.

                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0

    The uptake of AI technologies is often constrained by high initial investment costs, limited access to appropriate infrastructure, multidisciplinary experts and a lack of digital literacy within the sector.                   Methods · 4 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Method Type

            Methods
          

          
The research field and policy stance share a focus on case studies and computer vision, but the policy documents emphasize comparative policy analysis and machine learning. The research capabilities in multi-label classification and classification are not utilized by the policy evidence.
