Model Details

            Methods
          

          
The research field and policy stance share some commonalities, such as the use of LoRA layers for fine-tuning. However, there are gaps in the policy requirements not covered by the research capabilities.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 0Integration Feasibility · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2-LoRA model with LoRA for parameter-efficient fine-tuning
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Multi-label classification model ... LoRA layers Frozen vision foundation model
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Model architecture, settings, or analytical details not specified
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    ResNet-101 used as baseline (44.5M parameters), DINOv2-LoRA model with r=24 (5.91M parameters)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2-Giant architecture used as image encoder with frozen pre-trained weights; LoRA layers added to each Transformer block for fine-tuning.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · model_details · c007 · p.1
                  
    DINOv2-Giant architecture used as image encoder with frozen pre-trained weights; LoRA layers added to each Transformer block for fine-tuning.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset. Binary cross-entropy loss function was applied to each unit in the network output as a loss function.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · model_details · c010 · p.1
                  
    All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset. Binary cross-entropy loss function was applied to each unit in the network output as a loss function.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Techniques

            Methods
          

          
The research field and policy stance share some commonalities, such as the use of deep learning models and transfer learning capabilities. However, there are gaps in the policy requirements not covered by the research field, particularly in data pre-processing and evaluation methods.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 1Integration Feasibility · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2 vision foundation model with adapter learning, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Transfer learning capabilities assessment across seasons and habitats.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Data pre-processing (e.g., discretization algorithms, missing value imputation strategies and feature selection methods), k-fold cross-validation, repeated cross-validation, statistical tests (parametric and non-parametric), bootstrap-ping.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    VGG19-based model, InceptionV3-based model, self-supervised learning (SSL) methods including SimSiam, BYOL, MoCo-v3, Swin-Transformer-Base, ensemble model integrating Swin-S, Swin-B, and EfficientNet-B7
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c005 · p.1
                  
    Xu et al. (2024) applied several self-supervised learning (SSL)...ViT-B model with MoCo-v3 achieved the best performance...
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c005 · p.1
                  
    Shao et al. (2024) evaluated the performance of several SOTA deep learning architectures for multi-label classification...resulting in a match ratio of 63.33% and a macro F1 score of 81.74%
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2

Workflows

            Methods
          

          
The research field and policy stance share some commonalities, such as the importance of establishing an open-source dataset and applying LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2. However, there are gaps in the research capabilities not used, particularly in robust statistical validation techniques.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 1Reproducibility · 1Integration Feasibility · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Establishing an open-source dataset based on underwater photo transect surveys
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c003 · p.1
                  
    Establish an open-source dataset based on underwater photo transect surveys, annotate images by domain experts, apply LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Applying LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Robust Statistical Validation: train-test data splitting, hold-out estimator, k-fold cross-validation, repeated cross-validation
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Ground Truth Validation: comparison with another type of data or knowledge that can challenge our results beyond train/test data partition
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Transfer learning capabilities across seasons from dry season to wet season and vice versa
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Evaluating computational efficiency by comparing match ratios using different low-rank numbers (r)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Establishing an open-source dataset based on underwater photo transect surveys and applying LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2

                  Problem · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Primary Goal

            Problem
          

          
The research field's primary goal of introducing an approach integrating multiple models for coral reef condition classification aligns with the policy requirement to ensure trustworthy AI outputs through verifiable, standardised and traceable data sources. However, the research does not address the barriers to AI adoption in fisheries-dependent communities or ensure socio-economic viability of marine AI systems.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Integrating multiple approaches or models for coral reef condition classification
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Addressing barriers to AI adoption in fisheries-dependent communities
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Ensuring socio-economic viability of marine AI systems through economic, environmental, social, and legal considerations
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Verifiable, standardised, and traceable data sources for trustworthy AI outputs
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Ethical and inclusive AI technologies that are not only effective but also socially responsible
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Develop a comprehensive framework for trustworthy AI in marine research, fisheries management, environmental governance, and policy development
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Integrate economic, environmental, social, and legal considerations into the DINOv2 vision foundation model for coral reef condition classification
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Problem Statement

            Problem
          

          
The research field does not directly address the policy requirements, but it can be used as a starting point to develop more trustworthy AI systems that are transparent and accountable.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The work exists to address the gap in trustworthy AI deployment in marine biodiversity monitoring and management, ensuring transparency, validation, and ethical alignment with environmental and socio-economic goals.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Concerns about automation displacing jobs, particularly in small-scale fisheries where access to technical training and digital infrastructure is limited.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Addressing the gap in marine AI data governance, ensuring that data systems are ethical, transparent and interoperable.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Lack of enforceable safeguards for data governance and environmental accountability in AI development, reflecting capacity and infrastructure gaps.

Problem Types

            Problem
          

          
The research field focuses on monitoring, which aligns with some policy requirements. However, there are gaps in classification and decision support capabilities that need to be addressed.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1

    monitoring

    classification, decision support

  • 
          
    data governance, environmental accountability
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    classification
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Develop decision support tools based on monitoring data
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Establish data governance frameworks for environmental accountability
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Recommendations · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Future Work

            Recommendations
          

          
The research field focuses on developing a comprehensive framework for trustworthy AI that integrates marine research, fisheries management, environmental governance, and policy development. The combined policy stance emphasizes the importance of ensuring AI technologies are not only effective but also ethical and inclusive.

          

            Objective Overlap · 0Integration Feasibility · 0Institutions · 1Sustainability · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models for quantitative analysis
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · recommendations · future_work · c019 · p.1
                  
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Integrating continual learning techniques to enable adaptation to multi-year datasets or data collected from other regions
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    •

    Ensuring AI technologies are not only effective but also ethical and inclusive

Implementation Guidance

            Recommendations
          

          
The research field focuses on developing user-friendly interfaces for AI models, which aligns with the policy requirement to make AI development accessible. However, the research does not address the environmental impact of AI systems or workforce transition plans, which are key policy requirements.

          

            Objective Overlap · 0Integration Feasibility · 0Institutions · 0Sustainability · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Developing user-friendly interfaces to package AI models
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · recommendations · implementation_guidance · c005 · p.1
                  
    Open-source AI models, cloud-based solutions and cost-effective sensor technologies can provide alternatives to expensive proprietary systems, for example, in fishing vessels digitalization.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Evaluate environmental impact of AI systems, including energy use and sourcing
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Use interoperable data platforms and repositories to ensure transparency and accessibility in marine AI research
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Develop workforce transition plans to support shifts from manual to technical roles, ensuring equitable socio-economic outcomes
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Prioritize equitable access to AI tools and data, ensuring benefits are distributed across all stakeholders, including small-scale and marginalized communities
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Results · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Evaluation Metrics

            Results
          

          
The research findings on evaluation metrics do not directly address the combined policy stance on validation schemes and cost-benefit analyses. However, the research capabilities can be used to inform policy decisions with some bridging actions.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Usability · 1Ecological Impact · 0Social Impact · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Match ratio
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    F1 scores, Micro F1 score, Macro F1 score
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    State-of-the-art validation schemes (e.g., repeated cross-validation or bootstrapping); statistical tests for comparisons; ground truth testing
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Cost-benefit analyses, energy efficiency metrics, and workforce transition plans
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Statistical tests for comparisons; ground truth testing
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Cost-benefit analyses, energy efficiency metrics, and workforce transition plans
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Develop state-of-the-art validation schemes (e.g., repeated cross-validation or bootstrapping) to validate research findings
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Conduct cost-benefit analyses, energy efficiency metrics, and workforce transition plans to inform policy decisions
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Findings

            Results
          

          
The research findings provide a benchmark for the development of models in this field and serve as a practical tool for the accurate and efficient monitoring of coral reef conditions. However, there are concerns about automation displacing jobs and the need to address these issues through policy and governance frameworks.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Usability · 1Ecological Impact · 1Social Impact · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The DINOv2-LoRA model achieved superior accuracy with a match ratio of 64.77% compared to the best conventional model (60.34%).
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c001 · p.1
                  
    The DINOv2-LoRA model achieved superior accuracy with a match ratio of 64.77% compared to the best conventional model (60.34%). Incorporating LoRA reduced trainable parameters from 1,100M to 5.91M.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2-LoRA outperformed other models in most metrics and classes.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c011 · p.1
                  
    DINOv2-LoRA outperformed other models in most metrics and classes. It achieved the best performance in match ratio, micro F1, and macro F1 scores, with a significant reduction in computational cost compared to DINOv2.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Concerns about automation displacing jobs, particularly in small-scale fisheries where access to technical training and digital infrastructure is limited.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The uptake of AI technologies is often constrained by high initial investment costs, limited access to appropriate infrastructure, multidisciplinary experts and a lack of digital literacy within the sector.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Automatic, accurate, and efficient tool for coral reef condition analysis.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c019 · p.1
                  
    The DINOv2-LoRA model offers automatic, accurate, and efficient tool for coral reef condition analysis. It improves efficiency by reducing image labeling time from five seconds to 0.02 seconds.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Robust performance compared to other multi-label classification tasks based on ecological data.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c020 · p.1
                  
    The proposed model achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79%, demonstrating robust performance compared to other multi-label classification tasks based on ecological data.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Developing models that can accurately classify coral reef conditions and provide a benchmark for the development of models in this field.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c022 · p.1
                  
    Our findings provide a benchmark for the development of models in this field and serve as a practical tool for the accurate and efficient monitoring of coral reef conditions.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Fostering ethical, reliable, and policy-relevant AI systems in marine research, fisheries management, and environmental governance.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Stakeholders · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Actors

            Stakeholders
          

          
The research and policy both mention actors involved in the field, but the policy places more emphasis on the concerns of Fish and Fisheries, 2026.

          

            Affected Communities · 0Institutions · 1Accessibility · 0Usability · 0Social Impact · 1

                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.0

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    To better connect the research to policy use, consider involving Fish and Fisheries, 2026 in the research process.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.0
                  
    Xinlei Shao ∗†1, Hongruixuan Chen*2, Fan Zhao3, Kirsty Magson4, Jundong Chen5, Peiran Li6, Jiaqi Wang4 and Jun Sasaki1
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
            

          

        

        
        

          
            

              
Policy Comparison

              

              
The research field and policy stance share some commonalities, such as the importance of underwater surveys and sources of microplastic in the environment. However, there are also gaps between the two, particularly with regards to waste management practices and the development of standards for managing MPs. The research field focuses on underwater imaging and data extraction, while the policy documents emphasize systematic search methods, in situ sampling, and µFT-IR analysis. The research capabilities not used include transfer learning capability and pre-trained weights from ImageNet, LVD-142M dataset. The research field highlights inconsistencies in data quality and imbalanced distribution of the dataset, which are not explicitly addressed by the policy documents. The policy documents focus on the impact of microplastics on coral reefs, but do not provide information on data quality or limitations.

            

            

              
Pair-Level Fit

              
        

          
          
        

        
            

          
          

            

              
Field Calls18

              
Shared Features28

              
Policy Gaps21

              
Bridge Actions24

                   · data · collection_protocols · c018 · p.1

Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Requirements this policy asked for that the research still misses.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    On-site waste burning and 'collective collection,’’ method of waste management is the major source [126].
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Numerous groups have called for the development of standards for managing MPs due to concerns about their impact on the environment and the health of living organisms.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Systematic search method, manual review
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    In situ sampling, extraction, and µFT-IR analysis
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Data quality, coverage, or limitations not provided
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · data_quality · c026 · p.2
                  
    Yet, secondary MPs formed by breaking heavier plastic objects are substantial contributors, and even the proposed regulation mini­ mizes the existing levels of MPs throughout the ecosystem.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · data · data_quality · c028 · p.0
                  
    Studies have shown that exposure to environmental conditions, such as UV light and dust, causes the release of 1.5 million MPs mask materials [165,31].

Research Capabilities Not Used

          
Research Capabilities Not Used

          
Research strengths this policy does not yet capitalize on.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    Journal of Hazardous Materials 454 (2023), various research articles
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · data_sources · c002 · p.1
                  
    sources for coastal populations and act as key species to support the resilience and resistance of marine ecosystems (White et al., 2000). Coral reefs are also considered green infrastructure, protecting coastal regions from extreme weather such as storm surges and hurricanes (Brathwaite et al., 2022)
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Scopus, Web of Science, Sci-Lit, Google Scholar
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Transfer learning capability
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c009 · p.3
                  
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio. For experiments testing transfer learning capability, the dataset was split differently... All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset (Oquab et al., 2024). Subsequently, all models were fine-tuned using PyTorch.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Pre-trained weights from ImageNet, LVD-142M dataset
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c009 · p.3
                  
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio. For experiments testing transfer learning capability, the dataset was split differently... All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset (Oquab et al., 2024). Subsequently, all models were fine-tuned using PyTorch.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    High labor demands, low classification efficiency
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · data_quality · c002 · p.1
                  
    However, this approach has significant limitations, including high labor demands, low classification efficiency, and inconsistencies in data quality. These challenges arise because the entire process is highly dependent on the expertise and subjective judgment of human experts (Done et al., 2017; Figuerola-Ferrando et al., 2024)
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Images captured with different cameras and varying distances from the object

                   · data · data_quality · c021 · p.0
                  
    Images captured with different cameras and varying distances from the object, resulting in inconsistencies in image quality, color, and resolution.

    Development of standards for managing MPs due to concerns about their impact on the environment and the health of living organisms.

Accessibility

Data Quality

            Data
          

          
The research field highlights inconsistencies in data quality and imbalanced distribution of the dataset, which are not explicitly addressed by the policy documents. The policy documents focus on the impact of microplastics on coral reefs, but do not provide information on data quality or limitations.

The research field and policy stance share some commonalities, such as the importance of underwater surveys and sources of microplastic in the environment. However, there are also gaps between the two, particularly with regards to waste management practices and the development of standards for managing MPs.

                   · data · data_sources · c002 · p.1
                  
    sources for coastal populations and act as key species to support the resilience and resistance of marine ecosystems (White et al., 2000). Coral reefs are also considered green infrastructure, protecting coastal regions from extreme weather such as storm surges and hurricanes (Brathwaite et al., 2022)
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Implementation of waste management practices that reduce microplastic pollution.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Licensing

            Data
          

          
The research field's emphasis on open-source licensing does not align with the policy requirements, which prioritize copyright protection and reserved rights.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Accessibility · 0Reproducibility · 1Compliance · 0

                  Impact · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Ecological Impact

            Impact
          

          
The research improves efficiency by reducing labeling time and errors compared to inexperienced volunteers, but it does not address the complex and multi-faceted impact of microplastics on corals. The policy documents highlight the need for urgent steps to remove microplastics from the marine environment and minimize their harm to aquatic ecosystems and wildlife.

          

            Sustainability · 0Economic Impact · 0Ecological Impact · 1Social Impact · 0Institutions · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research improves efficiency by reducing labeling time and errors compared to inexperienced volunteers.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Microplastics can bioaccumulate in marine food chains, posing a risk to human health.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research does not address the complex and multi-faceted impact of microplastics on corals.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c014 · p.2
                  
    MPs can cause physical damage, smothering, and interference with essential biological processes in corals, reducing growth rates, reproduction, and overall health [69,68].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c014 · p.3
                  
    The presence of MP may endanger the coral ecology. Tiny MP may be misinterpreted for algae by marine species attributed to their range of hues [35].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c014 · p.4
                  
    Coral illness and MP pollution have proportional relation; once corals were polluted with plastics, the illness rate jumped by 489%. Coral habitats provide nutrition, coastal protection, recreation, and cultural meaning to over 275 million inhabitants, yet, increasing contamination has raised the danger of sickness throughout the coral habitat.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c017 · p.1
                  
    The overall consequence of MP on a zooxanthellate coral has yet to be understood [92]. Because of the growing acidity of the ocean, the survival of aquatic life as well as the ecological equilibrium of the global ocean is likely jeopardized. As CO2, through anthropogenic activities, interacts in seawater, it becomes more acidic, making it harder for some other marine creatures to survive and for corals to form their shells. This might lead to food cycle disturbances, ecosystem decline for various organisms, and coral bleaching.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c018 · p.1
                  
    This PE-MP may additionally hurt the oxidative system, inhibit the function of enzymes required for G. columna detoxification, increase the quantity of peroxidation inside the organism, and harm tissue by accumulating inside the coral medulla [32].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c018 · p.1
                  
    Moreover, MP may include chemical additions or pollutants hazardous to coral [147]. Such acute toxicity may impede the coral's capacity to collect and process food, decreasing eating effectiveness.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    Rising ocean acidity, jeopardized survival of aquatic life, food cycle disturbances, ecosystem decline for various organisms, coral bleaching.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.2
                  
    MP may be found across the cytoplasmic membrane of coral tendrils and inhabit Symbiodiniaceae dwelling sites, disrupting the expected relationships in both corals and host symbionts.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research does not explore the overall consequence of microplastics on a zooxanthellate coral.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c017 · p.1
                  
    The overall consequence of MP on a zooxanthellate coral has yet to be understood [92]. Because of the growing acidity of the ocean, the survival of aquatic life as well as the ecological equilibrium of the global ocean is likely jeopardized. As CO2, through anthropogenic activities, interacts in seawater, it becomes more acidic, making it harder for some other marine creatures to survive and for corals to form their shells. This might lead to food cycle disturbances, ecosystem decline for various organisms, and coral bleaching.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The research does not address the impact of microplastics on aquatic, terrestrial, atmospheric, and subterranean ecosystems.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.

    Develop a model that can predict the impact of microplastics on coral reefs.

In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.

    The presence of MP may endanger the coral ecology. Tiny MP may be misinterpreted for algae by marine species attributed to their range of hues [35].

    Coral illness and MP pollution have proportional relation; once corals were polluted with plastics, the illness rate jumped by 489%. Coral habitats provide nutrition, coastal protection, recreation, and cultural meaning to over 275 million inhabitants, yet, increasing contamination has raised the danger of sickness throughout the coral habitat.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c017 · p.1
                  
    The overall consequence of MP on a zooxanthellate coral has yet to be understood [92]. Because of the growing acidity of the ocean, the survival of aquatic life as well as the ecological equilibrium of the global ocean is likely jeopardized. As CO2, through anthropogenic activities, interacts in seawater, it becomes more acidic, making it harder for some other marine creatures to survive and for corals to form their shells. This might lead to food cycle disturbances, ecosystem decline for various organisms, and coral bleaching.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c018 · p.1
                  
    This PE-MP may additionally hurt the oxidative system, inhibit the function of enzymes required for G. columna detoxification, increase the quantity of peroxidation inside the organism, and harm tissue by accumulating inside the coral medulla [32].

    Moreover, MP may include chemical additions or pollutants hazardous to coral [147]. Such acute toxicity may impede the coral's capacity to collect and process food, decreasing eating effectiveness.

          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c022 · p.1
                  
    Numerous toxicity tests on coral varieties have been performed to learn about their diverse toxicological effects by subjecting them to critical environmental concentrations and forecasted increased proportions of MP.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Limitations · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Known Gaps

            Limitations
          

          
The research field and policy stance share a common goal of addressing gaps in understanding the impacts of microplastics on coral reefs. However, the research field focuses more on applying semantic segmentation models to images collected with quadrat for quantitative analysis, whereas the policy stance emphasizes the need for more research on the impacts of MP on the eating efficiency of Mediterranean stony coral species and the potential effects of MP on coral-feeding mechanisms.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Safety · 0Technical Maturity · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future research is needed to address gaps in understanding the impacts of microplastics on coral reefs.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · known_gaps · c021 · p.1
                  
    The adaptability of the model to broader regions outside the Indo-Pacific remains unexplored. Future research that uses coral reef images collected from other regions is essential to further evaluate the transferability of the proposed DINOv2-LoRA.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · known_gaps · c018 · p.1
                  
    Known gaps include the need for more research on the impacts of MP on the eating efficiency of Mediterranean stony coral species and the potential effects of MP on coral-feeding mechanisms.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The need for more research on the impacts of MP on the eating efficiency of Mediterranean stony coral species and the potential effects of MP on coral-feeding mechanisms.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · known_gaps · c018 · p.1
                  
    Known gaps include the need for more research on the impacts of MP on the eating efficiency of Mediterranean stony coral species and the potential effects of MP on coral-feeding mechanisms.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Applying semantic segmentation models to images collected with quadrat for quantitative analysis.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future research should adopt ecologically relevant circumstances to assess the long-term effects of MP exposure on varied coral species, particularly shoreline corals.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · known_gaps · c021 · p.1
                  
    The adaptability of the model to broader regions outside the Indo-Pacific remains unexplored. Future research that uses coral reef images collected from other regions is essential to further evaluate the transferability of the proposed DINOv2-LoRA.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · known_gaps · c020 · p.1
                  
    Future research should adopt ecologically relevant circumstances to assess the long-term effects of MP exposure on varied coral species, particularly shoreline corals.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Limitations Summary

            Limitations
          

          
This section highlights the limitations of the research study, which is imbalanced due to the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels. The policy documents emphasize the need for more research on the effects of MP exposure over time and the potential interface between ecological stresses associated with climate change and MP.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Safety · 0Technical Maturity · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0
                  
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, as shown in Figure 11, where the labels are distributed non-uniformly across classes, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The need for more research on the effects of MP exposure over time and the potential interface between ecological stresses associated with climate change and MP.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 3
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Proper disposal of plastic garbage offers a formidable obstacle.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The biomagnification parameters for greater trophic species and their effects on larger marine food webs are unexplored.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The overall effect of MP on a zooxanthellate coral has yet to be understood.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    More research is needed on the effects of MP exposure over time and the potential interface between ecological stresses associated with climate change and MP.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0
                  
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, as shown in Figure 11, where the labels are distributed non-uniformly across classes, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The overall effect of MP and other factors on coral reef habitats, like chemical contaminants, diseases, and climate-induced variations, should be considered.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.1
                  
    The overall effect of MP and other factors on coral reef habitats, like chemical contaminants, diseases, and climate-induced variations, should be considered.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    More research is needed to understand the impact of microplastics (MP) on coral reef ecosystems.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0
                  
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, as shown in Figure 11, where the labels are distributed non-uniformly across classes, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The need for proper disposal of plastic garbage should be acknowledged and addressed.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Methods · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Method Type

            Methods
          

          
The research field focuses on case studies, computer vision, and multi-label classification, which do not directly align with the policy stance on comparative policy analysis. The policy documents emphasize the need for a comprehensive approach to address plastic pollution, whereas the research field lacks this broader perspective.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 1Integration Feasibility · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Case study
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Computer vision
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Comparative policy analysis
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c003 · p.2
                  
    The design and reporting of this study followed the guidelines established by the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) framework [105].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c014 · p.1
                  
    The biomagnification parameters for greater trophic species and their effects on larger marine food webs are unexplored [25]. Concurrent consumption of micro-particles can change chromo­ somal, resulting in sterility, overweight, and cancers [130,151]. The presence of substances that mimic estrogen in women’s bodies has been linked to an increased risk of prostate cancer. Because humans are exposed to MPs through their meals, with a larger concentration seen in marine food items, this constitutes a substantial concern for consumer protection [160].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.1
                  
    Research revealed that PVC, PE, PET, and PA66 contact and consumption affected the immu­ nological function, antioxidant properties, mineralization, and energy consumption in Tubastrea aurea.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.2
                  
    Syakti et al. [150] assessed the consequences of three intentness (0.05, 0.1, and 0.15 g/L) and dimensions (100, 100–200, and 200–500 m) upon Acropora formosa’s viability.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.2
                  
    Whenever the future effects of two kinds of MP (63–125 m, 1 and 10 mg/L) above a 96-h exposition were evaluated, the hexacorallia reef Zoanthus sociatus was much more sensitive to PVC MP than LDPE,
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.2
                  
    Lanctˆot et al. [86] studied the effects of four weeks of exposure to 5 ± 1 particles/mL (mean ± SD; median = 4.67) and 70 ± 33 parti­ cles/mL PE MP on the stony coral Stylophora pistillata.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.3
                  
    Recent research on MP and its effects on coral organisms have been concentrated on scientific tests at concentrations greater than those observed in actual reef settings.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c021 · p.45
                  
    used nine distinct kinds of 3–5-millimeter MP to investigate the composition and features of MP surface pathogens in 4 coral reef locations.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c022 · p.2
                  
    Therefore different studies acknowledged several gaps, including MPs presence in wild corals [126], active or passive mechanisms of MP on coral tissue [143], stony corals’ in-situ reactions to MP [171], MPs psychological effect on corals [124] where other studies focused on prevalence, distribution, concentration, pollution of MPs on corals (Table 4).
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c022 · p.3
                  
    In this section, we aim to fill this gap by examining the policies and strategies adopted by different agencies and the existing recommenda­ tions for reducing plastic pollution.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c028 · p.0
                  
    Several organizations and governments have taken different initia­ tives to ban or reuse or recycle plastics to mitigate adverse impacts [34].
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    •

    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Investigate the effects of MP on corals in extended exposure
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Assess the consequences of three intentness and dimensions upon Acropora formosa's viability
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · method_type · c020 · p.2
                  
    Syakti et al. [150] assessed the consequences of three intentness (0.05, 0.1, and 0.15 g/L) and dimensions (100, 100–200, and 200–500 m) upon Acropora formosa’s viability.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Techniques

            Methods
          

          
The research field focuses on developing a DINOv2-LoRA model for underwater image classification, while the policy documents emphasize the importance of in situ sampling and extraction methods. The research capabilities not used by the policy include transfer learning capabilities assessment across seasons and habitats.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 1Integration Feasibility · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2 vision foundation model with adapter learning, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Underwater transect survey method, automated image classification based on universal assessment standards
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c002 · p.1
                  
    Underwater transect survey method, automated image classification based on universal assessment standards
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    In situ sampling, extraction, and µFT-IR analysis; trawl sampling for water and Van Veen grab sampling for sediment, stereotype microscope investigation, and employing ATR-FTIR.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Transfer learning capabilities assessment across seasons and habitats.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2-LoRA model, ResNet-101
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Developing a VGG19-based model and InceptionV3-based model for multi-label classification task.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Applying self-supervised learning (SSL) methods including SimSiam, BYOL, MoCo-v3, Swin-Transformer-Base, ensemble model integrating Swin-S, Swin-B, and EfficientNet-B7 for multi-label classification task.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c005 · p.1
                  
    Xu et al. (2024) applied several self-supervised learning (SSL)...ViT-B model with MoCo-v3 achieved the best performance...
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c005 · p.1
                  
    Shao et al. (2024) evaluated the performance of several SOTA deep learning architectures for multi-label classification...resulting in a match ratio of 63.33% and a macro F1 score of 81.74%
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Workflows

            Methods
          

          
The research field and policy stance share some commonalities, such as the importance of establishing an open-source dataset and applying LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2. However, there are gaps in the policy requirements not covered by the research capabilities, particularly in terms of study selection and screening.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 1Integration Feasibility · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Establishing an open-source dataset based on underwater photo transect surveys, annotating images by domain experts, and applying LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c003 · p.1
                  
    Establish an open-source dataset based on underwater photo transect surveys, annotate images by domain experts, apply LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Image classification followed by ecological information extraction
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    study selection and screening: identification, screening, eligibility, and inclusion
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Trawl and in situ sampling; extraction; Microscopic mechanism; FTIR spectroscopy analysis
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Transfer learning capabilities across seasons from dry season to wet season and vice versa.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Evaluating computational efficiency by comparing match ratios using different low-rank numbers (r)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Establishing an open-source dataset based on underwater photo transect surveys, annotating images by domain experts, and applying LoRA to fine-tune DINOv2

    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · workflows · c003 · p.1
