                  Problem · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Primary Goal

            Problem
          

          
The research field focuses on introducing an approach for multi-label classification of coral reef conditions, which does not directly address the policy requirements to understand and mitigate microplastic pollution.

          

            Topical Overlap · 0Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Understanding and mitigating the origins of MPs in reef ecosystems is crucial.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Problem Statement

            Problem
          

          
The research field and policy stance share a common concern about the impact of microplastic pollution on coral reefs. However, the research capabilities are not fully utilized to address the policy requirements.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Microplastic pollution is affecting aquatic ecosystems and coral reefs.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c002 · p.1
                  
    Microplastic (MP) pollution waste is a global macro problem, and research on MP contamination has been done in marine, freshwater, and terrestrial ecosystems. Preventing MP pollution from hurting them is essential to maintaining coral reefs’ ecological and economic benefits.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c002 · p.1
                  
    However, the public and scientific communities must pay more attention to MP research on the coral reef regions’ distribution, effects, mechanisms, and policy evaluations.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c014 · p.1
                  
    The biomagnification parameters for greater trophic species and their effects on larger marine food webs are unexplored [25]. Concurrent consumption of micro-particles can change chromosomal, resulting in sterility, overweight, and cancers [130,151]. The presence of substances that mimic estrogen in women's bodies has been linked to an increased risk of prostate cancer. Because humans are exposed to MPs through their meals, with a larger concentration seen in marine food items, this constitutes a substantial concern for consumer protection [160].
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c020 · p.1
                  
    The overall consequence of MP on a zooxanthellate coral has yet to be understood [92]. Because of the growing acidity of the ocean, the survival of aquatic life as well as the ecological equilibrium of the global ocean is likely jeopardized.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c020 · p.1
                  
    The processes of coral sensitivity to MP disclosure, such as proactive consumption and passive surface adherence, need to be investigated extensively. The overall effect of MP and other factors on coral reef habitats, like chemical contaminants, diseases, and climate-induced variations, should be considered.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Problem Types

            Problem
          

          
The research field's focus on monitoring is aligned with the combined policy stance, which emphasizes monitoring as one of its key areas.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    monitoring
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Develop monitoring frameworks that align with policy goals
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Recommendations · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Future Work

            Recommendations
          

          
The research field focuses on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models, which aligns with some policy requirements. However, there are gaps in addressing the mechanisms involved in MPs diversity and pollution, as well as understanding the sources and pathways of MPs.

          

            Objective Overlap · 0Integration Feasibility · 0Institutions · 0Sustainability · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · recommendations · future_work · c019 · p.1
                  
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Further study is required to understand the mechanisms involved in MPs diversity and pollution.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Alternative solutions for using chemical additives in microplastic production, such as biodegradation, should be explored.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Understanding the sources and pathways of MPs is critical for developing effective strategies to mitigate this global environmental problem.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Integrating continual learning techniques to enable adaptation to multi-year datasets or data collected from other regions.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Using numerical model simulations to improve the understanding of micro-plastic distribution and pathways in the marine environment.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · recommendations · future_work · c034 · p.4
                  
    Using numerical model simulations to improve the understanding of micro-plastic distribution and pathways in the marine environment.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.

                   · recommendations · future_work · c019 · p.1
                  
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    •

    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Implementation Guidance

            Recommendations
          

          
The research field focuses on developing strategies and initiatives to reduce MPs' environmental impact, which aligns with the policy stance. However, the policy requires more emphasis on hosting seminars and promoting responsible plastic use.

          

            Objective Overlap · 0Integration Feasibility · 0Institutions · 0Sustainability · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Developing strategies and initiatives to reduce MPs' environmental impact
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Hosting numerous seminars to promote an enabling environment and disseminate best practices
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Promoting the efficient and responsible use of plastic products by manufacturers and consumers
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    User-friendly GUI to package the model, making it accessible to non-professional stakeholders and conservation volunteers
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Developing a user-friendly GUI to package the model, making it accessible to non-professional stakeholders and conservation volunteers
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Results · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Findings

            Results
          

          
The research field and policy stance both highlight the importance of accurate and efficient monitoring of coral reef conditions, but the research field's findings on DINOv2-LoRA model can be used to improve coral reef condition analysis.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Usability · 1Ecological Impact · 0Social Impact · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research field and policy stance both highlight the importance of accurate and efficient monitoring of coral reef conditions.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c001 · p.1
                  
    The DINOv2-LoRA model achieved superior accuracy with a match ratio of 64.77% compared to the best conventional model (60.34%). Incorporating LoRA reduced trainable parameters from 1,100M to 5.91M.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c005 · p.1
                  
    Although these studies provided valuable information on coral reef health classification, limitations exist as they focused only on two to three classes...
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c011 · p.1
                  
    DINOv2-LoRA outperformed other models in most metrics and classes. It achieved the best performance in match ratio, micro F1, and macro F1 scores, with a significant reduction in computational cost compared to DINOv2.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c012 · p.1
                  
    DINOv2-LoRA demonstrates an exact alignment between the coverage and boundaries of the target features in the field images and its Grad-CAM heatmaps. It captures both the overall structure and subtle details of the target features, outperforming ResNet-101 and Swin-Transformer-Base.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c013 · p.1
                  
    DINOv2-LoRA offers a more balanced overall performance considering its computational efficiency. It achieved an optimal balance of transferability and accuracy across seasons with only 0.54% of the trainable parameters of the original DINOv2.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c014 · p.1
                  
    The performance of conventional deep learning models such as EfficientNet-B7, DenseNet-201, and HRNet-W48 experienced significant performance drops when transferred to the test set.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c015 · p.1
                  
    Vision foundation models (DINOv2-LoRA and DINOv2) outperformed conventional deep learning architectures in accuracy, transferability, and computational efficiency. DINOv2-LoRA consistently showed the best performance across various evaluation metrics.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c017 · p.1
                  
    The model incorrectly identified instances as being present when they are not, leading to false positives and false negatives.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c019 · p.1
                  
    The DINOv2-LoRA model offers automatic, accurate, and efficient tool for coral reef condition analysis. It improves efficiency by reducing image labeling time from five seconds to 0.02 seconds.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c020 · p.1
                  
    The proposed model achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79%, demonstrating robust performance compared to other multi-label classification tasks based on ecological data.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c022 · p.1
                  
    Our findings provide a benchmark for the development of models in this field and serve as a practical tool for the accurate and efficient monitoring of coral reef conditions.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c012 · p.1
                  
    Understanding the sources and pathways of MPs is critical for developing effective strategies to mitigate this global environmental problem.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c013 · p.131522
                  
    MPs can have multiple adverse effects on coral health, so understanding and mitigating the origins of MPs in reef ecosystems is crucial.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c014 · p.2
                  
    The detrimental impact of microplastics (MPs) on marine environments, particularly coral reefs, has become an increasingly concerning phenomenon over the past several decades.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c017 · p.1
                  
    Research revealed that PVC, PE, PET, and PA66 contact and consumption affected the immu­ nological function, antioxidant properties, mineralization, and energy consumption in Tubastrea aurea.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c018 · p.0
                  
    The presence of microplastics (MP) may endanger the coral ecology. MP can be misinterpreted for algae by marine species, and plastic trash may stimulate microbial proliferation that can result in hazardous infectious diseases in the waters.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c019 · p.0
                  
    MP can damage coral growth and kill corals in a variety of ways. Firstly, MP can physically injure coral tissues, interfering with eating and digestion, weakening the corals’ immunological mechanisms, and increasing their vulnerability to sickness, heat stress, behavioral change, and death [14,145]. Second, MP can introduce dangerous chemicals and pollutants into coral habitats, impairing coral viability and causing coral mortality.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c020 · p.131522
                  
    MP exposure affected the immunological function, antioxidant properties, mineralization, and energy consumption in Tubastrea aurea. LDPE MP caused coral whitening and death, exhibiting dose- and shape consequences. PVC MP disclosure at 10 mg/L significantly enhanced the plastic's adhesion to coral shells and produced mitochondrial dysfunction.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c031 · p.1
                  
    The critical review focuses on how microplastic pollution affects coral reefs. The review examined where microplastics are found around the world, where they come from, and what is known about how they affect coral and human health.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research field does not address the distribution, sources, and characteristics of microplastics in coral reefs.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail

                   · results · findings · c012 · p.1

    Although these studies provided valuable information on coral reef health classification, limitations exist as they focused only on two to three classes...

    DINOv2-LoRA outperformed other models in most metrics and classes. It achieved the best performance in match ratio, micro F1, and macro F1 scores, with a significant reduction in computational cost compared to DINOv2.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c012 · p.1
                  
    DINOv2-LoRA demonstrates an exact alignment between the coverage and boundaries of the target features in the field images and its Grad-CAM heatmaps. It captures both the overall structure and subtle details of the target features, outperforming ResNet-101 and Swin-Transformer-Base.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c013 · p.1
                  
    DINOv2-LoRA offers a more balanced overall performance considering its computational efficiency. It achieved an optimal balance of transferability and accuracy across seasons with only 0.54% of the trainable parameters of the original DINOv2.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c014 · p.1
                  
    The performance of conventional deep learning models such as EfficientNet-B7, DenseNet-201, and HRNet-W48 experienced significant performance drops when transferred to the test set.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c015 · p.1
                  
    Vision foundation models (DINOv2-LoRA and DINOv2) outperformed conventional deep learning architectures in accuracy, transferability, and computational efficiency. DINOv2-LoRA consistently showed the best performance across various evaluation metrics.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c017 · p.1
                  
    The model incorrectly identified instances as being present when they are not, leading to false positives and false negatives.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c019 · p.1
                  
    The DINOv2-LoRA model offers automatic, accurate, and efficient tool for coral reef condition analysis. It improves efficiency by reducing image labeling time from five seconds to 0.02 seconds.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c020 · p.1
                  
    The proposed model achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79%, demonstrating robust performance compared to other multi-label classification tasks based on ecological data.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · results · findings · c022 · p.1
                  
    Our findings provide a benchmark for the development of models in this field and serve as a practical tool for the accurate and efficient monitoring of coral reef conditions.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    To better connect the research capability to policy use, policymakers can incorporate the findings on DINOv2-LoRA model's accuracy and efficiency into coral reef conservation strategies.

                  Stakeholders · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Actors

            Stakeholders
          

          
The research field focuses on a team of authors, while the policy documents mention various organizations and institutions. The research capabilities are not directly applicable to the specific industries mentioned in the policy documents.

          

            Affected Communities · 0Institutions · 0Accessibility · 0Usability · 0Social Impact · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research field focuses on a team of authors, while the policy documents mention various organizations and institutions.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.0
                  
    Xinlei Shao ∗†1, Hongruixuan Chen*2, Fan Zhao3, Kirsty Magson4, Jundong Chen5, Peiran Li6, Jiaqi Wang4 and Jun Sasaki1
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.1
                  
    a Department of Geography and Environmental Science, Begum Rokeya University, Rangpur 5400, Bangladesh b Department of Geography, Chang’An University, Xi’An 710064, Shaanxi, China c Department of Disaster Management, Begum Rokeya University, Rangpur 5400, Bangladesh d Department of Soil Science, Patuakhali Science and Technology University, Dumki, Patuakhali 8602, Bangladesh e Department of Development Studies, Daffodil International University, Dhaka 1216, Bangladesh f Interdisciplinary Institute of Indian System of Medicine (IIISM), Directorate of Research, SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST), Kattankulathur 603203, Tamil Nadu, India g Department of Fisheries and Marine Science, Faculty of Science, Noakhali Science and Technology University, Noakhali, Bangladesh h Department of Chemistry, College of Science, King Khalid University, Abha 62529, Saudi Arabia i Research Center for Advanced Materials Science (RCAMS), King Khalid University, Abha, Saudi Arabia j Department of Agro-food Safety and Crop Protection, National Institute of Agricultural Sciences, Rural Development Administration, Jeonju, Republic of Korea k Post-Graduation Program in Conservation of Cerrado Natural Resources, Goiano Federal Institute, Urutaí, GO, Brazil l Post-Graduation Program in Ecology, Conservation, and Biodiversity, Federal University of Uberlˆandia, Uberlˆandia, MG, Brazil m Post-Graduation Program in Biotechnology and Biodiversity, Federal University of Goi´as, Goiˆania, GO, Brazil
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c024 · p.131522
                  
    Numerous groups have called for the development of standards for managing MPs due to concerns about their impact on the environment and the health of living organisms.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research field does not address the specific industries mentioned in the policy documents (manufacturers, cosmetic and pharmaceutical industries).
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.0
                  
    Xinlei Shao ∗†1, Hongruixuan Chen*2, Fan Zhao3, Kirsty Magson4, Jundong Chen5, Peiran Li6, Jiaqi Wang4 and Jun Sasaki1
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    To better connect the research capability to policy use, consider highlighting the importance of interdisciplinary collaboration between authors and organizations.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.0
                  
    Xinlei Shao ∗†1, Hongruixuan Chen*2, Fan Zhao3, Kirsty Magson4, Jundong Chen5, Peiran Li6, Jiaqi Wang4 and Jun Sasaki1
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c024 · p.131522
                  
    Numerous groups have called for the development of standards for managing MPs due to concerns about their impact on the environment and the health of living organisms.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
            

          

        

        
        

          
            

              
Policy Comparison

              

              
The research field and policy stance share a focus on underwater surveys and fieldwork, but the policy requires more comprehensive action plans and national-level research. The research field focuses on underwater photo-transect survey method, while the policy documents emphasize establishing networks and organizing information infrastructure. The research capabilities are not fully utilized in the policy context. The research field highlights inconsistencies in data quality and imbalanced distribution of the dataset, which are not explicitly addressed by the policy documents. However, the policy documents emphasize the importance of caution in coral transplantation and monitoring the status of a bill, which are not directly related to the research findings.

            

            

              
Pair-Level Fit

              
        

          
          
        

        
            

          
          

            

              
Field Calls18

              
Shared Features24

              
Policy Gaps23

              
Bridge Actions23

    However, this approach has significant limitations, including high labor demands, low classification efficiency, and inconsistencies in data quality. These challenges arise because the entire process is highly dependent on the expertise and subjective judgment of human experts (Done et al., 2017; Figuerola-Ferrando et al., 2024)
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · data · data_quality · c021 · p.0
                  
    Images captured with different cameras and varying distances from the object, resulting in inconsistencies in image quality, color, and resolution.

Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Policy Requirements Not Covered

          
Requirements this policy asked for that the research still misses.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    The Action Plan was developed by the Working Group of Action Plan for Coral Reef Conservation (WG-APCRC) organized by the Ministry of the Environment (MOE), in cooperation with other national and local government agencies, and academic societies including Japanese Coral Reef Society (JCRS)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    MOE and JCRS, 2004
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Continue the existing survey and monitoring programs that aim to capture the status and transition of coral reef ecosystem
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Establishing a nationwide network that shares information about local challenges and existing conservation activities.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Extreme caution in selecting transplant location, species, and intra-specific strains for coral transplantation
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Monitoring the status of a bill and taking actions based on its idea where appropriate

Research Capabilities Not Used

          
Research Capabilities Not Used

          
Research strengths this policy does not yet capitalize on.

          

            
    
        
  • 
          
    sources for coastal populations and act as key species to support the resilience and resistance of marine ecosystems (White et al., 2000). Coral reefs are also considered green infrastructure, protecting coastal regions from extreme weather such as storm surges and hurricanes (Brathwaite et al., 2022)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · data_sources · c002 · p.1
                  
    sources for coastal populations and act as key species to support the resilience and resistance of marine ecosystems (White et al., 2000). Coral reefs are also considered green infrastructure, protecting coastal regions from extreme weather such as storm surges and hurricanes (Brathwaite et al., 2022)
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · collection_protocols · c009 · p.3
                  
    The dataset was divided into training, validation, and test sets in a 7:1:2 ratio. For experiments testing transfer learning capability, the dataset was split differently... All conventional deep learning models were initialized with pre-trained weights from ImageNet, while DINOv2 was pre-trained on the LVD-142M dataset (Oquab et al., 2024). Subsequently, all models were fine-tuned using PyTorch.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Field images collected during dry and wet seasons at 15 diving sites in Koh Tao, Thailand
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Domain experts annotating images following coral reef conservation standards
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    High labor demands and low classification efficiency in data quality process
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · data · data_quality · c002 · p.1
                  
    However, this approach has significant limitations, including high labor demands, low classification efficiency, and inconsistencies in data quality. These challenges arise because the entire process is highly dependent on the expertise and subjective judgment of human experts (Done et al., 2017; Figuerola-Ferrando et al., 2024)
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Long-term observation at selected, representative ecosystems
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    •

Licensing

            Data
          

          
The research and policy findings align on open-source licensing, which is a key aspect of data sharing and accessibility.

          

            Transparency · 1Auditability · 0Accessibility · 1Reproducibility · 0Compliance · 0

                  Impact · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Ecological Impact

            Impact
          

          
The research improves efficiency in labeling images, which is not directly related to the policy's focus on coral reef ecosystem conservation. However, the research can be used as a tool to support conservation efforts.

          

            Sustainability · 0Economic Impact · 0Ecological Impact · 1Social Impact · 0Institutions · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Improving efficiency in labeling images from five seconds (volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (model)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Identifying mechanisms that trigger coral degradation and loss
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Long-term observation at selected, representative ecosystems
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Establishing Marine Protected Areas for corals of biologically significant areas and providing restoration projects
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c020 · p.1
                  
    In line with the universal classification standard used in citizen science-based conservation programs (Scott, 2019), the proposed DINOv2-LoRA model significantly improves efficiency by reducing the time required to label an image from five seconds (when done by a trained volunteer) to 0.02 seconds (when processed by the trained model). In addition, inexperienced volunteers are prone to errors, whereas DINOv2-LoRA achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79% on the all-season dataset.
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · impact · ecological_impact · c016 · p.1
                  
    Designating the biologically significant areas as Marine Protected Areas (MPAs) has been recognized by the international community as one of the most important conservation measures.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Limitations · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Known Gaps

            Limitations
          

          
The research field highlights the need for future work to address known gaps, which aligns with the policy requirement to share experience and information among groups. However, the research capabilities not used in this section could be bridged by applying semantic segmentation models to coral reef images collected from other regions.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Safety · 1Technical Maturity · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Experience and information obtained from [coral transplant projects] are yet to be shared among other groups.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The adaptability of the model to broader regions outside the Indo-Pacific remains unexplored. Future research that uses coral reef images collected from other regions is essential to further evaluate the transferability of the proposed DINOv2-LoRA.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · known_gaps · c021 · p.1
                  
    The adaptability of the model to broader regions outside the Indo-Pacific remains unexplored. Future research that uses coral reef images collected from other regions is essential to further evaluate the transferability of the proposed DINOv2-LoRA.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.

Limitations Summary

            Limitations
          

          
The research field's limitation on dataset imbalance is not explicitly addressed by the policy, highlighting a potential gap in sharing experience and information among organizations.

          

            Transparency · 0Auditability · 0Safety · 1Technical Maturity · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0
                  
    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, as shown in Figure 11, where the labels are distributed non-uniformly across classes, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Experience and information need to be shared more among different organizations for a better conservation and collaboration efforts.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1

    The dataset used in this study is imbalanced, with the class Healthy Coral (HLC) comprising the highest number of labels.

                   · limitations · limitations_summary · c020 · p.0

                  Methods · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Method Type

            Methods
          

          
The research field and policy stance share a focus on case studies, but the policy documents emphasize comparative policy analysis, which is not explicitly addressed in the research. The research capabilities in computer vision are not utilized by the policy documents.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 0Technical Maturity · 0Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 0Integration Feasibility · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    case study
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  •

Techniques

            Methods
          

          
The research focuses on developing and fine-tuning deep learning models for coral reef conservation, while the policy emphasizes practical conservation actions. The research capabilities not used by the policy include advanced computer vision techniques and self-supervised learning methods.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Technical Maturity · 1Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 1Integration Feasibility · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2 vision foundation model with adapter learning, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Underwater transect survey method, automated image classification based on universal assessment standards
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · methods · techniques · c002 · p.1
                  
    Underwater transect survey method, automated image classification based on universal assessment standards
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    removal of crown-of-thorns starfish, coral transplant, removal of coral predators, coral monitoring programs, coral aquaculture, live culture of seedling, support for local communities
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    VGG19-based model, InceptionV3-based model, self-supervised learning (SSL) methods including SimSiam, BYOL, MoCo-v3, Swin-Transformer-Base, ensemble model integrating Swin-S, Swin-B, and EfficientNet-B7

Workflows

            Methods
          

          
The research field and policy stance share the importance of establishing networks and organizing information infrastructure among various interested and responsible parties domestically and internationally. However, the research capabilities for transfer learning across seasons and evaluating computational efficiency are not explicitly addressed in the policy documents.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 0Technical Maturity · 0Interoperability · 0Reproducibility · 0Integration Feasibility · 0

                  Problem · 3 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Primary Goal

            Problem
          

          
The research field's primary goal is to introduce an approach integrating the DINOv2 vision foundation model with the LoRA fine-tuning method for multi-label classification of coral reef conditions. This aligns with the policy stance on promoting conservation and sustainable use of coral reefs, while balancing their development and local communities' needs.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Multi-label classification of coral reef conditions
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Balancing sustainable use and development of local communities
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    DINOv2 vision foundation model and LoRA fine-tuning method
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Integrate DINOv2 vision foundation model with LoRA fine-tuning method for coral reef conservation
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Problem Statement

            Problem
          

          
The research highlights the challenges of processing complex underwater ecological images, which is relevant to the policy's focus on coral reef ecosystem conservation. However, the policy does not explicitly address these challenges, and experience and information need to be shared more among different organizations for a better conservation and collaboration efforts.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The research highlights the challenges of processing complex underwater ecological images, which is relevant to the policy's focus on coral reef ecosystem conservation.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · problem · problem_statement · c001 · p.1
                  
    Conventional deep models struggle to achieve high performance when processing complex underwater ecological images.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    •

    The policy does not explicitly address the challenges of processing complex underwater ecological images, which is a key aspect of coral reef ecosystem conservation.

Problem Types

            Problem
          

          
The research field focuses on monitoring, which is partially aligned with the policy stance on conservation and monitoring. However, there are gaps in data governance and restoration/decision support areas that need to be addressed.

          

            Topical Overlap · 1Objective Overlap · 0Scope Overlap · 0Urgency Alignment · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1

    Monitoring

    Conservation, data governance

    Data governance
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1

    Develop data governance framework to support conservation and monitoring efforts
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Recommendations · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Future Work

            Recommendations
          

          
The research field focuses on future work in using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models, which aligns with the policy's emphasis on establishing networks and organizing information infrastructure. However, the research capabilities not used include integrating continual learning techniques to enable adaptation to multi-year datasets or data collected from other regions, which is relevant to the policy's requirement for sharing experience and information among different organizations.

          

            Objective Overlap · 0Integration Feasibility · 1Institutions · 0Sustainability · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Future work could focus on using images collected with quadrat and applying semantic segmentation models if quantitative analysis is required.

                   · recommendations · future_work · c013 · p.2
                  
    Developing networks among research groups, museums, experts, and NGOs to share biological databases, research findings, and successful case studies.

Implementation Guidance

            Recommendations
          

          
The research field's focus on developing a user-friendly GUI to package the model aligns with the policy stance on promoting conservation and sustainable resource use of the coral reef ecosystem. However, there is a mismatch in the level of detail provided by the research field compared to the policy documents.

          

            Objective Overlap · 0Integration Feasibility · 0Institutions · 1Sustainability · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Developing a user-friendly GUI to package the model, making it accessible to non-professional stakeholders and conservation volunteers.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Establish networks and organize information infrastructure among various interested and responsible parties domestically and internationally, in order to promote conservation and sustainable resource use of the coral reef ecosystem.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Contribute to the establishment of local socioeconomic framework that benefits from appropriate use and management of healthy coral reef ecosystems.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Connect organizations for cooperation, MOE will invite responsible ministries, agencies, and experts, and hold liaison conferences periodically to discuss issues regarding conservation and sustainable use of coral reef ecosystem.

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    MOE will plan exhibitions that highlight the value of coral reefs and call action for its conservation. Also, at various reefs including those in Iriomote-Ishigaki National Park, MOE will continue to plan and support nature-friendly field activities open to local residents and visitors.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    MOE will collect and organize information on coral reef researches and conservation efforts and strengthen organizational and systematic structures. MOE will fully utilize data from their programs such as National Survey on the Natural Environment and Monitoring Sites 1000.

                  Results · 2 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Evaluation Metrics

            Results
          

          
The research provides valuable insights into the performance of the model, but it does not directly address the policy requirement for quantitative indicators. To bridge this gap, the research capabilities can be used to develop such indicators.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Usability · 1Ecological Impact · 1Social Impact · 0
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Match ratio, Micro F1 score, Macro F1 score
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    quantitative indicators such as the number of MPAs and their size
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Micro F1 score, Macro F1 score
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Develop quantitative indicators for MPAs and their size to complement the research findings
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
        

          

            
Findings

            Results
          

          
The research field and policy stance share a common goal of promoting conservation and sustainable use of coral reef ecosystem, but the research capabilities are not fully utilized in the policy framework.

          

            Methodological Relevance · 1Usability · 1Ecological Impact · 1Social Impact · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The DINOv2-LoRA model offers automatic, accurate, and efficient tool for coral reef condition analysis.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c019 · p.1
                  
    The DINOv2-LoRA model offers automatic, accurate, and efficient tool for coral reef condition analysis. It improves efficiency by reducing image labeling time from five seconds to 0.02 seconds.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Vision foundation models (DINOv2-LoRA and DINOv2) outperformed conventional deep learning architectures in accuracy, transferability, and computational efficiency.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c015 · p.1
                  
    Vision foundation models (DINOv2-LoRA and DINOv2) outperformed conventional deep learning architectures in accuracy, transferability, and computational efficiency. DINOv2-LoRA consistently showed the best performance across various evaluation metrics.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Policy Requirements Not Covered · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    Establish a nationwide network that shares information about local challenges and existing conservation activities.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c009 · p.1
                  
    Establish a nationwide network that shares information about local challenges and existing conservation activities.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Designating the biologically significant areas as Marine Protected Areas (MPAs) has been recognized by the international community as one of the most important conservation measures.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Research Capabilities Not Used · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The model incorrectly identified instances as being present when they are not, leading to false positives and false negatives.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c017 · p.1
                  
    The model incorrectly identified instances as being present when they are not, leading to false positives and false negatives.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    Understanding the significance of coral reef ecosystem in cultural and societal dimension is important.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
                

                  Bridge Actions · 2
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    The proposed model achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79%, demonstrating robust performance compared to other multi-label classification tasks based on ecological data.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c020 · p.1
                  
    The proposed model achieved a micro F1 score of 88.05% and a macro F1 score of 83.79%, demonstrating robust performance compared to other multi-label classification tasks based on ecological data.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        
        
  • 
          
    The DINOv2-LoRA model achieved superior accuracy with a match ratio of 64.77% compared to the best conventional model (60.34%). Incorporating LoRA reduced trainable parameters from 1,100M to 5.91M.
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · results · findings · c001 · p.1
                  
    The DINOv2-LoRA model achieved superior accuracy with a match ratio of 64.77% compared to the best conventional model (60.34%). Incorporating LoRA reduced trainable parameters from 1,100M to 5.91M.
    
                
    
                
    
          
    
        
    
        
        
    • 
        

                  

                

                
        

        
                  

                

                
                

                  Stakeholders · 1 field(s)
                  

                    
        

          

            
Actors

            Stakeholders
          

          
The research field focuses on the actors involved in coral reef conservation, which aligns with the policy stance that emphasizes the importance of various organizations and stakeholders. However, the research does not explicitly address the need for MOE's involvement in promoting ecotourism activities and best management practices.

          

            Affected Communities · 1Institutions · 1Accessibility · 0Usability · 0Social Impact · 1
          

          
                

                  Shared Features · 1
                  

                    
    
        
  • 
          
    actors
    
          
    
            
          
    
          
        
    
          Evidence Trail
          
    
            
    Research evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c001 · p.0
                  
    Xinlei Shao ∗†1, Hongruixuan Chen*2, Fan Zhao3, Kirsty Magson4, Jundong Chen5, Peiran Li6, Jiaqi Wang4 and Jun Sasaki1
    
                
    
                
    Policy evidence
    
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c002 · p.1
                  
    The Action Plan was developed by the Working Group of Action Plan for Coral Reef Conservation (WG-APCRC) organized by MOE, in cooperation with other national and local government agencies and academic societies.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c006 · p.1
                  
    The Action Plan was developed by the Working Group of Action Plan for Coral Reef Conservation (WG-APCRC) organized by the Ministry of the Environment (MOE), in cooperation with other national and local government agencies, and academic societies including Japanese Coral Reef Society (JCRS)
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c010 · p.1
                  
    the government will support biodiversity conservation and restoration activities initiated locally, including those in MPAs that are of high importance, and areas that are integral part of Ecological Network21. Okinawa Coral Reef Conservation Council, while pursuing reef conservation efforts, will connect themselves and strengthen partnership with various responsible and interested parties.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c010 · p.1
                  
    Okinawa Prefectural Government will draft a base plan for conservation, restoration, and utilization of coral reefs (provisional title “Okinawa Base Plan on Conservation, Restoration, and Utilization of Coral Reefs”) and promote such activities; will monitor growth and health of coral reefs; assess the contributing factors that are negatively affecting reefs, and come up with measures considering the unique features of the area.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c011 · p.1
                  
    MOE will work together with other responsible countries to formulate ICRI East Asia Regional Strategy on MPA, and pursue proposed actions accordingly. MOE will support research and educational functions at Palau International Coral Reef Center (PICRC), founded by grant assistance of Japanese Government
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c011 · p.1
                  
    JCRS will work together with other relevant groups towards establishment of Asia-Pacific Coral Reef Society (provisional title)
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c012 · p.1
                  
    specialists are highly sought after, not only experts knowledgeable about coral reef ecosystem and engineers with special skills,
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c013 · p.1
                  
    MOE will collect and organize information on coral reef researches and conservation efforts and strengthen organizational and systematic structures, including the functions of COREMOC as a clearinghouse.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c013 · p.2
                  
    MAFF will collect and organize scientific information on rare species, and develop conservation and management plans.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c013 · p.2
                  
    Kagoshima Prefectural Government will launch a long-term monitoring program at fixed stations that periodically collects data on the coverage and growth of
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c014 · p.1
                  
    Okinawa Prefectural Government will asses the status of coral reefs in Okinawa... using data from various sources such as MOE’s National Survey on the Natural Environment and Okinawa’s Reef Check projects...
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c015 · p.1
                  
    MOE will implement pilot monitoring programs on the coastal water quality and biological assessments, some of which call for public participation; draft a manual to adopt Sato-Umi concept and promote projects in the local communities based on the idea; disseminate Sato-Umi concept domestically and internationally (mostly within Asian region) through Symposiums and other public relations activities.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c015 · p.1
                  
    MAFF will support conservation efforts, e.g., coral monitoring programs initiated by the local fishermen and residents, coral aquaculture including live culture of seedling, coral transplant, and removal of crown-of-thorns starfish; will provide technical support as well as disseminate information on the lessons learnt from excellent case studies.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c016 · p.1
                  
    MOE will promote plans and programs developed in compliance with the Act on Promotion of Ecotourism enacted in April 2008; will create awards for excellent ecotourism activities and best management practices (BMPs), and plan nationwide seminars on the issue; and accumulate and share information regarding better local resource use and effective conservation methods.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c016 · p.1
                  
    At the local level, based on the Act, Kerama Area in Okinawa established Ecotourism Promotion Committee with the members from various organizations including people from Zamami- and Tokashiki Villages as leading groups.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c017 · p.1
                  
    MOE and other responsible ministries and agencies will promote establishment of MPAs for coral reefs using applicable regulations with clarification about MPA based on the Basic Plan on Ocean Policy.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c019 · p.1
                  
    MOE will closely monitor the status of the bill, while taking actions based on the idea of the bill where appropriate. MAFF will support local environmental protection projects that reduce sediment runoff, including slope modification and installation of sediment ponds at agricultural lands.
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c019 · p.1
                  
    MAFF will try to reduce residential runoff that negatively affects marine organisms by installing improved drainage systems in the upland areas. MAFF will promote conservation of biodiversity through an integrated watershed management approach,
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c019 · p.1
                  
    MLIT will continue its efforts regarding the treatment of night soil and domestic effluent by employing sewage systems and septic tanks. It will also take measures to improve water quality,
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c019 · p.1
                  
    Tokyo Prefectural Government will implement measures to reduce the impact of feral goats that excessively graze in upland areas in Ogasawara Islands, and minimize sediment runoff,
    
                
    
                
                
    
                   · stakeholders · actors · c019 · p.1
                  
    Okinawa Prefectural Government will implement mitigative measures to tackle red soil runoff from development projects based on the Red Soil Erosion Prevention Ordinance,
